text:
Gelato (ジェラート, Jerāto) is a member of the Heil-Ly Family. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 36, Chapter 378 

Appearance

Gelato is a woman of average height. The right side of her hair is dark, while a portion of the left side is light. However, the rest of the left side is dark and longer than the right side. She also appears to have suture/stitch marks on both of her eyebrows.

Personality

From the description of her occupation, Gelato seems to enjoy gaming. She tends to keep to herself, playing video games at the family table instead of joining her fellow members in conversation. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 38, Chapter 399 She is extremely obedient to Morena, seen standing beside her during the card game. Like her fellow Heil-Ly members, she has sadistic tendencies and no qualms about killing people. She seems to be cautious, saying that it would be risky to kill in the open because of the stricter security. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 38, Chapter 393 

Plot

Succession Contest arc

Gelato is seen with the other Heil-Ly members as Morena uses her Contagion on them one at a time and explains how her Nen ability works. 

In the Heil-Ly's hideout, several members look at the bottom half of Luini's corpse up against a nearby wall. A construction worker named Daemon (level 20) points out how much Luini's loss will affect them since he allowed them to hunt for prey from the hideout. Gelato (a professional gamer and level 19) adds how dangerous it will be now since the military is on high alert. An investigator's assistant named Perigord (level 18) suggests that someone should recreate Luini's ability. An arcade employee named Bille (level 20) says that he has already decided on his ability. Perigord reminds them that Morena said everyone has an innate type, confident that Luini was an Emitter. Bille replies that an Emitter needs to do the job, but Gelato points out that they need to figure out how to raise their levels so they can reach level 21. An Emitter and human trafficker named Voconte (level 26) suggests using his "door" ability to catch prey that wander into the trap, adding that they have ways to reel them in. 

Tevelares and Quorolle then enter the room, with the latter wondering if there are any Emitters since they need someone who can create projectiles. As Perigord comments on the popularity of Emitters, a college student named Matvere (level 18) happily says that he wants to be an Emitter and plans to make a firearm, with Gelato commenting that he doesn’t get to choose. Quorolle asks if he could handle handcuffs turned into pigeons, leaving Matvere confused. After Tevelares explains that the Xi-Yu underboss killed Padaille with his ability, and they need to destroy the pigeons at a distance, Matvere replies that he would need to try it to know for sure. Gelato, Tevelares, and Quorolle then enter a separate room and explain the situation to Morena, who emphasizes the need to analyze their opponents and use creativity. She explains that they don't need to rely on an Emitter if they can restrict the enemy or their ability. She then gives the example of an ability that tells one how many hits are needed to defeat an enemy, comparing it to a game. Quorolle realizes that she's the game master for a reason, and Gelato says that she will keep the idea in mind if she turns out to be a Conjurer. Morena asks if she wants to know her type, but Gelato replies that she wants it to be a surprise. 

After being teleported to the Heil-Ly's hideout, Tassi is left confused when he finds himself standing in front of a closed door. Bille suddenly appears behind him and stabs him in the neck with a small knife, celebrating the fact that he reached level 21. Three other members congratulate him as Matvere hands him a cup to perform the Water Divination test and says that he was a Transmuter. Bille hopes to be an Emitter, but is left disappointed when he turns out to be a Conjurer. Gelato is envious of him and points out that there seem to be a lot of left-handed Conjurers. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 38, Chapter 394 

As Hinrigh and Nobunaga infiltrate the Heil-Ly's hideout, Gelato has a conversation with Soufflé and Terebellum about something. She is also seen looking at a piece of paper as she talks to the other two members. 

On the twelfth day of the voyage, Morena notifies some of her underlings that 9 th Prince Halkenburg's funeral procession is taking place, and that it would be a perfect opportunity as a lot of people will gather for the send-off, and says that Dogman finding the person they were looking for is a green light and the beginning of the end. Bille cheers in excitement, but Gelato expresses hesitation at whether the person Morena seeks will join us if they know of their goals. Morena acknowledges this, and Gelato cheers her on. Hunter × Hunter, Chapter 405 

The Heil-Ly capture PFC Borksen during her patrol as Halkenburg's funeral procession passes by her on Tier 3. She is brought to the Heil-Ly Family Secret Hideout, where she regains consciousness only to find herself surrounded by Morena and five of her underlings, including Gelato. Gelato stands on Morena's immediate right with her arms folded across her chest, facing Borksen as Morena explains the rules of a negotiation game to Borksen. Hunter × Hunter, Chapter 407 

Gelato watches silently as Morena and Borksen play their game. After Borksen discards the "Yes" card in the second round, Gelato is disappointed and does not try to hide her expression. From the reactions of the Heil-Ly to the "Yes" card being discarded, Borksen concludes that they likely had not cheated in the past, though she cannot rule out that she is the first person they have tried to recruit that does not want to join them. The speaker beeps and interrupts the game to announce that Special Martial Law has been declared. Hunter × Hunter, Chapter 408 Despite the declaration of Special Martial Law, Borksen continues the game. Gelato continues spectating, and watches intently during the final round to see whether Borksen's final card would be a "No" or a "Return", which she had earlier stated would be used to return the "X" card and void the game. Borksen flips over the "Return" card, and crumples it, having grabbed it too tightly in her relief at surviving. Gelato and the other underlings leave, not believing they need to watch Borksen return the "X" card, but hear Morena ask Borksen if she had made a mistake, and turn to see that Borksen has chosen to return the "Yes" card. Hunter × Hunter, Chapter 409 Morena's tone changes, and she accuses Borksen of cheating. Gelato and the other underlings return and surround her. Morena reveals that cheating in the card game triggers a manipulative ability that forces its targets to say only Yes or No, and hears Borksen affirm one more time that she will join the Heil-Ly. Gelato and the other underlings applaud her choice, and Borksen is released from the manipulative ability, collapsing to the ground. Hunter × Hunter, Chapter 410 

Abilities & Powers

Nen

Having reached level 19 with Morena's Nen ability, Gelato's aura amount and output have increased. 

Manga Appearances

Trivia

Gelato is named after the dessert called Gelato. She shares a similar origin of her name with every other female member of the Heil-Ly Family: Cashew, Chiffon Toto, Montblanc Toto, and Soufflé.
